column36: Started with ultimate nulifier in the hq, so I knew I wanted one player to save up for that. So I was getting copies of Focus Chi and over drive. Living Weapon came out, then the next turn I was able to setup guaranteed draw 4 from it by buying Ultimate Nulifier. Eventually that player also got bfg and sentient starship. Focus chi was consistently hitting 0, 3-9, and with Ultimate Nulifier I could focus that into hitting Apocalypse without worrying about his tactics. I know I won't be that lucky again, so for the first time in a tournament I'm submitting my first attempt without trying for a second.
Only bystander I didn't save was tucked under Famine. I hope that everyone struggled with the city a bit... hate to be the only one with 2 escapes...

column36: Brutal setup, only saved by wallcrawl on everyone. Got one deck to getting BFG every turn with "boom goes the dynamite" and lots of draw, and still got hit by Undounted and Undefeated three times in a row.

Really tough but fun game! Heaviest I’ve leaned into shards for one of these games and definitely helped. So many turns where I just scraped together enough attack to keep the city at bay, never had less than 3 in the city generally it felt relentless! Had to hit apocalypse only 7 times which is always a win. Thanks so much Kylah for organising this! Always so much fun!

Though setup! Our first try with Colossus did not go as expected, so we changed for Invisible Woman for our second try. Much better! We were lucky to draw Immortal and Undefeated as the 4th tactic, and probably saved a few turns because of that.
The only SL card to show up was his rare. At least no escapes.

column36: This is the exact same mastermind and scheme (and therefore both villains as well) combo from week 3 of the last tournament. We’d prefer to see a greater degree of randomness, rather than the reuse of these themed scenarios (and not enough themed about melk).

Ooof, that was a tough one! We got absolutely hammered in game 2 by 3 strikes in 4 turns right towards the end, with Pestilence escaping in the middle of that to discard so many good cards.
Game 1 was going a similar way due to not seeing a Henchmen until like turn 12, but it swung hard to us when I hit Chameleon above Jean's rare. This game me enough attack to hit another villain, giving breathing room, and a news reporter drew me the recruit I needed to buy it. From that point on I was off to the races.
Star Lord was incredibly useful, as his artifacts came into play straight away and we made great use of Legendary Outlaw to copy both Shards and draws. It was a real frustration putting 3 Element Guns and a Chip on top of my deck after a Strike! 
Despite the win, both my wife and I found this a little too un-fun, but that was mostly the Horsemen and Apocalypse KOing non-greys at the worst time.

column36: Easily the most challenging setup for me the whole tournament! In the first rounds the city was pretty rough, I had 2 escapes already for round 10. But I was able to pull off a pretty great combo really early in the game with Fixit's and Caiera's 4 costs, which gave me 9 points (even with the 4 others being starter heroes) to defeat strong (Phalanx-Infected) villains. I used that combo twice to gain 2 rare heroes, and for turn 21 I had 4 rares for Player1! Gamora's rare was really great to always be Worthy and to always weaken the MM just by existing as an artifact. The last turn I got 88 attack points with Drax's and Caiera's rares double-double-ing my points, which was great, but it came a little late since I only had to attack the MM once. Still pretty fun though.

It was a challenging game but really fun to play. Even when we lost on the first game before hitting the MM during the Final Blow. She Hulk was great to give us cards that cost 6 and Thor with the Mjolnir we were able to win the second game after a tough start were we had too many escapes.

This was difficult and hard to maintain. Getting enough shards to be able to fight the Mastermind proved too difficult with the city being overran since the very beginning. Able to manage to draw out the deck with one more hit remaining on the Mastermind. Slow, drawn out death but not sure what else could be done.

Pizza just kept pumping Extra cheese with dr strange and accidentally went wasp. Extra cheese has all the cheering crowds and draw. In our first game, we got shafted by 5 scheme twists in a row which leads to 4 escapes. Thankfully second game totally hulk came in clutch and ended the bargaining. This scheme was very swingy for tournament play ie 5 scheme twists in a row leading to escapes. Demonic bargain is great for tournament play. Lots of variety and strategy for who to bargain.

First I just have to say, how much I dislike playing without Sidekicks.  So many turns I'd have 2 recruit and nothing to do with it.  This was kind of an annoying setup to play, between Dormammu making me discard and Giants of Jotunheim constantly making me mill my deck (thus making me shuffle way more than usual).  Also, in the game I'm not submitting (2nd game).  I chained 4 scheme twists in one turn.  Causing 3 Villains to escape.  So even though it was my fast game with more points per turn, I had to go with the game with no escapes -- obviously.
Artifacts are always fun to play with. They can ramp up quickly. The What If ... side had too much variation and was hard to hit.

Our second game took longer than the first due to being bogged down in wounds (too prideful to bargain with demons) but were able to stave off escapes and get more victory points. Thought wrong that Gamora might help us more with the bargains. Slow developing synergy in game 2 as well. We probably should have kept Ms. Marvel in game 2 to bump up covert and card draw.

Both matches were a bit swingy. My first attempt went better, but even in this attempt I drew a chain of 5 consecutive twists causing 4 Escapes in a row. Nevertheless fun to play and a worthy final match! Thank you for doing this tournament. It's huge and a lot of work! I really appreciate this! :-)

Good first game, unlucky second. Love size changing and card draw with these heros. Ended up having a turn where was able to fight Dr. Strange rare and recruit Ms Marvel and Wasp rares plus four other heroes in one turn. Great tournament. Thanks for all the time and energy everyone put into this.

column31: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts
column33: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts
column36:
My fun rating is based on attempt 2, as attempt 1 was a bad luck fest. Deck 1 was already really well underway to super strength thanks to Werewolf by Night's 3-cost but the Twists came too soon and the villains revealed were never really in attack range. Only 13 turns and 7 total VP.

Attempt 2 was no super quick game, but it was more fun, as I this time was able to only KO one quantum realm villain (the first was still too strong early on). I was able to buy 2 rares but never drew them. Blood Frenzy was super strong, as I improved my Victory Pile to generate 6 attack (or recruit) per instance. The final turn I had 17 attack, enough to defeat Ghost in both transformed sides plus 1 quantum hound. It was quite funny/annoying that the second copy of the weak (2 attack) Reaver was defeated so many times over the course of the game, but never entered a victory pile. It didn't end up mattering in my game, but I had a question come up: Would each instance of fighting him trigger as a 'defeat' or only if he doesn't discard a card with recruit? Especially the combination with Werewolf by Night's 3-cost would care about it for KO-ing. I'll ask this question in the discord as well, I want to know it for a next occasion.

Keeping the Quantum realm villains at bay proved much easier than in the failed attempt 1. It still was a struggle, but it was definitely more doable. There was plenty chance to KO between Werewolf By Night and the Quantum Hounds. Also Explore was a really fun keyword to use. It wasn't super strong, but it helped getting into Moonlight sometimes. Also I noticed I didn't recruit even a single Janet van Dyne card. She feels like the weakest and least interesting hero in this setup, so I'm not surprised by that, but usually every available hero gets at least some cards into play.

Thank you to Kylah and Bageltop for their behind the screens efforts to manage and organize this tournament. It's always a nice experience with some surpirses along the way. My results weren't that impressive over the 7 weeks, but I did win my pod 2 times, so that's a nice feeling. I find that choosing the right heroes is a skill I get better at over time, but it's certainly not my forte yet. I sometimes enjoy to simply choose heroes I enjoy a lot. In those caes the games are fun and that's more important to me than winning. But seeing all the data and results from everybody is a nice addition and I guess we all learn from every tournament and setup we play.

I am glad that I don't have any deadlines anymore, as that was sometimes pretty hard to juggle over a week. I'll probably take a short break from Marvel Legendary and then come up with some setups to play on my own, without competition. See you all in the next tournament!

column36: Never was able to get the undercover engine going. Won almost solely due to Iron Fist's focus chi.  If you have 9 recruit every turn, you only need 4 attack. Wish I had a game where the HQ wasn't half size by turn 6 but that's the way it happened.

Lost first game as the Heli carrier blew up in flames near the end...we found our hands a little clogged with wounds, so we switched out domino for bishop to give us additional ways to destroy those pesky wounds. The game went much smoother the second time around as one player fully embraced the power of Phil Coulson shield levels. The other player got a lot of power synergies between iron fist and variable card costs which helped garner recruit and attack to help shatter the MM. All in all a great set up and very challenging! great tournament! thanks so much Kylah!

it still got VERY close tho, i checked when counting 'cards in VD left' and next turn would've been the last twist. i also believe this might be the first time i played against exodus but the scheme was far more dangerous even though his high atk stat.

I gotta say, Exodus sucks.  And my deck building was not great.  Between him and the Acolytes and my hero choices with very little recruit power I was struggling.  My first game started with three strikes right off the bat to put several wounds in my deck before I could blink.  After that it was a steady stream of villains filling up the city.  I couldn't afford to let my few recruitable guys get knocked out, so the city had me focused and I was only able to get Exodus twice before the last scheme popped up in ...turn 28!?!?!  I had a significant villain deck left to work thru when the game ended.  It was a bummer.  Luckily my second game I was able to build a good Coulson deck with the Rare Siryn for some mastermind shattering.  Once my 6-cost Coulson's had a stack of undercover agents to deal out some big damage with it was clear sailing, but I was right up to the wire on that helicarrier exploding.  Still, I would fight exodus again with a different scheme.  Nice job making week 7  a nail-biter.

Exodus can be so tough, and this was no exception.  I think the first game with Deathlok could have gone better with more luck, but Strong Guy was a much better selection for game 2, making it possible to avoid excessive wounds by discarding Absorb Kinetic Energy.  Still came down to the wire, and would have lost on the next turn.

Took mockingbird for shield level, gamora for shards and red synergy. Shieldy and shardy hands. 
Interesting start. 2 master strikes. Meaning 2 wounds each. But at least had 5recruit and 6 recruit starting hands, which mitigates the bad luck a bit. Unfortunately there were 2 more strikes early, so had to sacrifice some turns for the heals. Could grab Groot and Syrin rares. The latter is a beast actually. (And in this setup the Groot one as well. With Groot and Gamora cards had lots of shards. But it was too late when it could get going. Shield was also lagging behind unfortunately. Could get 4 hits in Exodus, but got the 8th twists on turn 36, so I just missed the final blow.. Exodus (and acolyte villains with him) are nasty. They take up your money and Random can screw your turn. Mastermind tactics are nasty as well with two of them putting villains on the streets. Ended up with full villain row..

column2: One Planet's Trash is Another Celestial's Treasure!
column36: Fun game to finish up with. I always enjoy playing ego, though we had wins in both games we also had very early escapes in both games due to twists and strikes. We don't play co-ordinate very often so that was fun too. Thanks again.
Brutally tough setup -- just when you think you have everything under control... 3 scheme twists in a row to ruin your day. Thanks for a great tournament!

Crazy and fun setup! The hero options were a lot to consider, and I was excited to play with material I hadn't yet. I ended up trying Gamora (MCU) and Ultimate Spider-man because Gamora's kit was versatile, she had a 2-cost card, and I was very intrigued to try her Triggered Artifact with a spider hero. (My first time playing her at all.) Within the setup, I saw Rocket & Groot and Peter's Allies also had 2-cost cards which encouraged me to try Ultimate Spider-man. I thought Black Panther's draw card and "number of multi-color cards played" card would be nice in the Spider-man deck as well, but in hindsight I should have probably just kept it to all 2-cost cards as they did brick some momentum sometimes. From the other deck, I was really impressed with Michelle from Peter's Allies. I had not played with that hero group before, and it woke me up to the power within! All the recruit with Gamora's artifact and card draw recruit cards made for a very effective way to fight with recruit while getting to recruit sweet cards along the way. Super fun hero group. I tried Maximus in a second attempt to defeat Henchmen for free after seeing how big the MODOKs could get with Striker and the recurrence of Ghost Racers, but as it turned out my first attempt without Maximus was better. His non-cheese defeat for free cards were actually hurting the rest of the game plan. This scheme and mastermind didn't allow the luxury of city space to add henchmen back to the city by choice. My win was a long game which came very close to losing, but it culminated in an amazing turn for Ultimate Spider-man and Gamora's artifact, like I hoped. I generated over 80 attack, over 20 recruit, fought Ego 7 times, and fought several heroes to clear the way. I had to break out a calculator (rather than my usual spindown dice) because Ultimate Spider-man's rare alone created 42 attack! Super fun way to finish out another really fun tournament. Great setups and variety this tourney, and I had a ton of fun trying a lot of components I hadn't tried before. Cheers!

If I was to rate this out of ten, it would be a 9.6. point two points for two groups of rise of the living making this setup much harder, and another two for trying to remember things like: you can only coordinate one card per turn, M.O.D.O.K's have striker, or remembering that there can be multiple master strikers under multiple master strikes (AKA ascended masterminds master strikes get ignored accidentally more often than it should). 

Other than that, this is as fun as it is challenging. With all the KO provided by the heroes and villains decks were thinned quickly, as well as the superior helpfulness of the Peter's allies hero set. Even on my first game when I was losing, I was still having fun. Now that may be because I've almost beaten hollow knight, but really this game felt like a boss from hollow knight where they feel very fair but challenging, and you can easily see where you messed up. 

So this game is almost a perfect setup, and it would be a perfect 10 game if either there was one less rise of the living making dead group, or if rocket/Groot was not a preselect. (Their hero set isn't bad, it's just their cards were much lower value compared to the other options l).

Only allowing 6 villains to escape with the Rise of the Living Dead was brutal. I lost my first game after a very short time because of that. Second game gor extremely lucky with only 1 Master Strike revealed (so striker wasn't bad) and some lucky Rise draws after killing someone else. Gamora artifacts with lots of drawing allowed me to swiftly kill Ego when he got small.

This was tough, but really fun.  We were able to use Happy's Danger Sense well to KO a couple master strikes, which was probably the difference between win & loss.  Wife used Crystal & Panther to pull off some great turns, while I went more unconventional using Michelle to use recruit to hit villians & mastermind.
I love EGO!  This one kicked my butt.  Super bad shuffles.  Hate to end the tourney with a showing like this.  I had fun with this one and will be replaying it beat it.

The first game had too many early Strikes and Twists that caused escapes. The second game had too much Rise of the Living Dead that caused escapes (of the 23 Villains and Henchmen left in the Villain Deck, only 6 had Rise). In neither game did I get the Hero cards I wanted to make the decks work. A pair of games that perfectly exemplified how bad this game can be sometimes when you are simply at the whim of the pre-game shuffle.
Thank you for hosting this. I had a great time.

The first round we focused a lot of deck building and it was super fun even though it took us 38 turns, red guardian and wasp comboed nicely. 

Second round we buckled down and hit the mm much faster, but had an unfortunate escape. Opted to go with it just because we were so much faster
Not a bad set up. I think our game could have gone better, however our second game was worse. Fun way to end the tournament. Thank you for the set ups and we look forward to the next one!

good setup. Went all tech and it worked but the escapes were annoying. 2 in game 1 and 1 in game 2. Second game was 1 turn slower but 1 less escape and more VP. Left did lots of draw and big ant man stomp and right had a crazy turn at the end with 13 sidekicks played and 2 magik uncommons.

column36:
I had a 4-2 split my first five turns. And all six twists showed in the first half of the game. Of course I had so many escapes. That said I want to tell you a story about turn 27.
Player 1 built a magic/wasp deck with one ant-man card. It was giant ego. 
Turn 27 I played 10 sidekicks. Giant ego had 22 attack on its own. This turn I had a total of 48 attack and played every card in the deck, which was 20 cards. I even had 3 sidekicks I couldn’t play. Cleared the city, the goblins, and 3 MM tactics. Turn 28 player two managed enough attack with its 2 rares to finish the game.

We got pretty unlucky at the beginning of both games, drawing a lot of henchman and schemes right off the bat and flooding the city. I think we messed up our first game and didn't draw another card from the villain deck after a scheme twist, so we're going with the secone round, which came down to the absolute wire. I managed to get a lot going with superior iron man and some ant man/magik mixed in, my wife was doing her thing with fantastic four united and brainstorm. Overall it was a lot of fun though, it took us a minute to get off the ground, but once we did we crushed it.

Lots of fun mechanics at play here -- Mysterio is always a unique mastermind that's not really a problem until you realize the villain deck is running low and you have eight tactics to go through, and Hidden Witnesses allow even useless hands to contribute something. Our first hero selection slaughtered us, but switching for more S.H.I.E.L.D. and Investigate made for a much more playable game!

In hindsight we should have risked an escape or two and hit the mastermind earlier, but the scoring really punishes escapes and we didn't want to risk it.

The Soul Gem was also clutch to have enough attack to hit Thanos though it's a shame that I could not burn the shards on it to hit Thanos earlier.

Major serious ouch.

Really fun setup. For my first time facing an epic mastermind, I think I did pretty good, at least for me. Lost both games but both were very fun. I think I hit Romulus 3 times in each. The one scheme Psi-lord is fun for, so I had to bring him. Both times, I got hit by two master strikes but never let a third one go off, Psi-lord k.o.ed the other three. It was also very fun to have a villian deck w/ two cards, one being a bystander, and have Psi-lord repeatedly rescue them. Managed to control the villian decks, avoid all the scheme twists, but just couldnt get enough power. Really fun, though, imo.

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36:
It was a challenging setup, but a fun puzzle to try to crack. We took advantage of the scheme's focus ability to get the Scheme Twists to the bottom of as many dimensions as possible, giving us as much time in this scheme as we could muster. We were able to hit Romulus 4 times, but we came up just short of hitting his face card for the final blow. His Weapon X Sequence made his attack just high enough, that even when me and my partner looked at every possible way to max out our attacks for the turn we were a few attack points shy of defeating him... GG Romulus, maybe next time!

Thanks for organizing another fun (and challenging) tourney for everyone. We look forward to the next one!

Not gonna lie...my second attempt at this setup was not enjoyable. Not because it wasn't a solid setup, but because for the second tournament in a row I've been completely destroyed by this game. Couldn't seem to get a decent result on any given week for the life of me. Once again going to finish near or at the bottom of the full tournament standings. Just feel frustrated. I know I'm not the best at this game, but boy does it ever feel deflating to not have anything go your way week after week. Makes me start questioning if this game is even for me. I'm glad this is the final week because right now I'm just not in the mood to try another setup. I'm sure this feeling will fade after a bit...but the fun factor definitely faded away during this last playthrough and I just wanted to get it over with. Not how I ever thought I'd feel after playing Legendary. This is in no way a reflection on the people who have spent tireless amounts of time building this tourney (very, very grateful for your efforts) nor is it a reflection on the game itself. It's just how I'm feeling in this moment after another (far) less than hoped for tournament result.

Tried to do Weapon X Sequence on one hand and Berserk on the other. In both games, I never wound up pulling enough Fantomex cards to get Sequence working and had too many Agents hanging around for proper Berserking. Six thumbs down.

just awful games both times , really ending the tourny in a slump, oh well. ggs guys

column36: There were so many rules text that kept us on our toes. Once we managed to keep the “Rise of the living dead” under control, the game was a bit smoother. Lots of fun and frustration!

The first battle was a hard fought tie. I changed up my heroes and Killmonger Special Ops and Apoc Black Widow made the bystanders really flow.  I won second game in turns 41 on a nice 21pt combo that knocked out the Witch. Im lucky I didn't have any odd number heroes!

The zombies were too resilient. No matter how hard we fought, they had a tendency to come back. The battle was a long slog and in the end, Zombie Scarlet Witch could not be stopped even though her Frost Giants were sufficiently held back.

column36: I never felt good about this setup from the get-go.  I really needed to select someone with soulblind so I could have prevented the zombies keep returning to the city.  We never even sniffed attacking Scarlet Witch.  Ugh.  Lesson learned.
N/A
i thought i might as well risk it until i get a lucky shuffle, and it happened game2. Won by getting Storm's and Cyclops' rares same hand.
Black widow is the only choice. Second choice was Emma Frost to push a couple extra villains to the city. First game ended up being the best. I enjoyed the bonus game. Great idea!

This was crazy hard! The only reason we won: about mid point of our game, the destroyer villain (we used marvel studios phase 1 version enemies of Asgard) was on the rooftops and, with the help of storm’s “lightning bolt”, was able to defeat the card and destroy all his shield heroes. At this point, player 2 had a hand of storm’s “lightning bolt”, and three essential capt America cards (perfect teamwork, avengers assemble, and his rare “a day like any other”) amongst a couple other avengers cards. With this in hand, it was essentially a hit to LOKI every one of his turns. However, we STILL just barely made it with 1 WOUND LEFT TO GO for defeat. CRAZY!

I was originally going to rank this as a 2, because it is just a BRUTAL setup.  However, from a tournament stand-point I do enjoy the moving parts and the required strategy to manage the city and scheme.  You have to build your hero deck really strategically.  You have to determine when you want to hit something in the city.  Do you try to rush the game and try to hit Loki ASAP and ignore the scheme?  Or do you try to plan around avoiding the wounds and building up?  There are so many variables and avenues to take.  So I've revised my rating to a 4 but only because of the above reasons.  The setup itself is really unfair if you're playing for fun.  :-)

I thought that Spider-Man would offer a cheap method to avoid wounds from everything but the Enemies of Asgard and this proved to be the case.  It was a tough game and I almost had an escape but a "lucky" Master-Strike happened and despite getting down to 1 wound in the deck, I was able to hit Loki to end the game 2 turns later.  This is a tough setup and avoiding both the wounds and escapes is a tricky situation.
Though setup! Capt. America Diving Block saved the day many times and helped managing the Scheme, MM and Villains asking for Blue cards.
We used our third attempt, which had the same hero’s as our second. Enjoyed this one! Thank you for setting up these tournaments! Deeply appreciated!

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36: Usually I hate overconstructed setups like this. But in this rare case the Base Set alone became thrilling again! After a really long considering I finally decided to choose the very versatile Cap and Natasha for additional VP and her Tech cards. I couldn't believe my eyes when I discovered Diving Block in the HQ on turn 1 and having 6 Recruit for P2! Diving Block saved my day! But there was a nasty moment, when I took Loki's third tactic to defeat a Villain for free! I only had the Endless Army of Hydra in the city, so I actually had to play 2 additional cards from the Villain Deck causing several wounds!! I was close to a loss! But P2 somehow also managed to obtain A day unlike any other and that with the that card I could made 26 attack in my final turn and could defeat Loki twice with only 2 Wounds left in the Wound Pile. Ooof! No need to try this match again. I don't think I would be that lucky again!

First game, lots of early Strikes, Twists, and other Wound-inspiring things came early; I held out until turn 22, but a loss was writ on every flip of the Villain Deck.  Second game, those cards came later and a Victory was secured, so a third game was thankfully unnecessary.

I picked the game I played with my son (third attempt). I predict the escapes will cost us but it was nice to be able to play with him in this!
Cap was the hero here - Diving Block did so much work in all three games.

This was a lot to handle for a game of Core. Can see where the difficulty could lay in. Two of the three games we got unlucky with shuffling. But our second game had a nice and drawn out engine with Iron--Man and Hawkeye working together to draw through an entire deck, and give the Deadpool/odd style deck enough draw power as well to get hits frequently on the city of the Mastermind. Even with all that luck, it came down to us still having 1 wound left in the deck.
Results are for Game 3. For game 2, I used Cyclops and Iron Man.

It was fun, but had to give it a three based on the fact that I don't really want to play it again.  If I ever want a really hard challenge, though, this is the go-to.  I marked my recorded attempt as my second, but it was really my third.  For my second I tried Hulk and Iron Man.  I figured I just wouldn't ever play the green card that gave everyone a wound unless it was enough to put me over the top on my last turn and there were at least three Wounds left.  That scenario did not arise.  Captain America in the end was the card that made the difference.  One of my players was able to recruit his Uncommon card once and it actually allowed me to make extra draws instead of taking a Wound on two occasions.  I actually figured this was unlikely to happen, I really wanted to try Captain America more for his +1 per color since both players were going to have to have as much Tech/Strength/Ranged as they could get.  Figuring it would be a good payoff -- which it was.

Lost two of three times. Just too many wound risks to avoid and so we rated fun level low. Just were fortunate with one winning game because there 10+ wounding cards left in villain deck and player 2 had strong hand of tech cards.
Took me til my third attempt to beat it. I drew a Twist on turn 1 on all 3 attempts.

Game 3 submission here. Cap and Tony again. First two games went poorly. Third game, I managed to get very lucky with the timing of most of the Wound-giving cards later in the game. Cap's uncommon and rare were clutch for protecting me and wailing hard on Loki. The setup is slightly redeemed in my eyes, but I still think Kylah hates us.

Brutal.  Liked Hulk and Iron Man, but their blue and green and even tech were buried deep in the hero deck.  The scheme twists came out slowly enough, but they got me in the end.  Just couldn't get the greens and techs and blues to show up when I needed them.  And there was no way to get rid of starters (except by way of Destroyer).

Definitely the most challenging core MM - Scheme setup. I've tried to beat this setup many times with my sister during Covid, we played tens of games for hours. But when you can chose all the heroes, it is a lot easier, this was really hard. Maybe I'll try a few more games before giving up :)
Challenging setup which was surprising for core - went 0-3 but came within the final blow in the 3rd try! Thanks for the tourney! Fun to get back to core set for a challenge.

With the scheme, mastermind and villain combo there was just too many necessary card colors to prevent wounds. Our thought was to go for a quick kill and rack up Black Widow's bystander count. We took on far too many early wounds to ever mount a serious challenge. Pretty unsatisfying way to go out. For fun we ended up playing a 4th (and 5th game) and mixed up the heroes with Captain America and Cyclops thinking they'd offer more protection. Also not successful. We're sure there's a way to win this, but it seems like you have to be extremely lucky.  Always fun to play. Look forward to the Spring 2026!
Woof.  Someone has to be pretty lucky to win this setup.

Yep, I'm really bad at the game lol. This was a really fun challenge, though. First game, I totally was paying attention and didn't forget what Loki's master strike ability was after setting up and starting lol. Second and third attempts I got cooked. Prob a bad approach. Third attempt was really unlucky, though. A lot of early twists and strikes. I kinda want to keep trying till I figure out how to win but I think that'll take too long lol. This was fun to do, though.

Tried widow to just rush down loki then cap to try and mitigate wounds. Both games I was halfway through the wounds stack before I even had a chance at avoiding any wounds(3 wounds to each side in the first 4 turns). Didn't feel like I even had a chance.

brutal set-up!, many villains you can't be or need to avoid defeating, didnt get much going on and the stack just emptied out, 28 turns the first try seemed bad but the other tries got a lot less turns in before the end of the game so couldnt get much going since it also flooded the deck.
Lost all 3 matches. Game 2 was close. If I had about 2 more turns . I would have won.  All villains/twists/schemes.. making you gain wounds... I almost chose Hulk... just because.
Attempt #3. Each better than the last, but early wounds hurt.

I didn't think you could make this wicked of a setup with only Core! Very dastardly of you - I think the chosen heroes are part of the complication. 
We are intentionally submitting our 3rd game which was the worst to see how low on the totem pole we can rank - as that was an epically bad game for us!
